heading: Medicare coverage gap discount program
label: 1395w–114a Medicare coverage gap discount program

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: Subject to subsection (h), the Secretary shall establish a Medicare coverage gap discount program (in this section referred to as the “program”) by not later than . Under the program, the Secretary shall enter into agreements described in subsection (b) with manufacturers and provide for the performance of the duties described in subsection (c)(1). The Secretary shall establish a model agreement for use under the program by not later than 180 days after , in consultation with manufacturers, and allow for comment on such model agreement.January 1, 2011March 23, 2010

children.heading: Agreement
children.label: A Agreement
children.text: An agreement under this section shall require the manufacturer to provide applicable beneficiaries access to discounted prices for applicable drugs of the manufacturer.

children.heading: Provision of discounted prices at the point-of-sale
children.label: B Provision of discounted prices at the point-of-sale
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (c)(1)(A)(iii), such discounted prices shall be provided to the applicable beneficiary at the pharmacy or by the mail order service at the point-of-sale of an applicable drug.

children.heading: Special rule for 2011
children.label: i Special rule for 2011
children.text: In order for an agreement with a manufacturer to be in effect under this section with respect to the period beginning on , and ending on , the manufacturer shall enter into such agreement not later than not later than 30 days after the date of the establishment of a model agreement under subsection (a).January 1, 2011December 31, 20111So in original. Second “not later than” probably should not appear.1

children.heading: 2012 and subsequent years
children.label: ii 2012 and subsequent years
children.text: In order for an agreement with a manufacturer to be in effect under this section with respect to plan year 2012 or a subsequent plan year, the manufacturer shall enter into such agreement (or such agreement shall be renewed under paragraph (4)(A)) not later than January 30 of the preceding year.

children.heading: Provision of appropriate data
children.label: 2 Provision of appropriate data
children.text: Each manufacturer with an agreement in effect under this section shall collect and have available appropriate data, as determined by the Secretary, to ensure that it can demonstrate to the Secretary compliance with the requirements under the program.

children.heading: Compliance with requirements for administration of program
children.label: 3 Compliance with requirements for administration of program
children.text: Each manufacturer with an agreement in effect under this section shall comply with requirements imposed by the Secretary or a third party with a contract under subsection (d)(3), as applicable, for purposes of administering the program, including any determination under clause (i) of subsection (c)(1)(A) or procedures established under such subsection (c)(1)(A).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: An agreement under this section shall be effective for an initial period of not less than 18 months and shall be automatically renewed for a period of not less than 1 year unless terminated under subparagraph (B).

children.heading: By the Secretary
children.label: i By the Secretary
children.text: The Secretary may provide for termination of an agreement under this section for a knowing and willful violation of the requirements of the agreement or other good cause shown. Such termination shall not be effective earlier than 30 days after the date of notice to the manufacturer of such termination. The Secretary shall provide, upon request, a manufacturer with a hearing concerning such a termination, and such hearing shall take place prior to the effective date of the termination with sufficient time for such effective date to be repealed if the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.label: I
children.text: if the termination occurs before January 30 of a plan year, as of the day after the end of the plan year; and

children.label: II
children.text: if the termination occurs on or after January 30 of a plan year, as of the day after the end of the succeeding plan year.

children.heading: Effectiveness of termination
children.label: iii Effectiveness of termination
children.text: Any termination under this subparagraph shall not affect discounts for applicable drugs of the manufacturer that are due under the agreement before the effective date of its termination.

children.heading: Notice to third party
children.label: iv Notice to third party
children.text: The Secretary shall provide notice of such termination to a third party with a contract under subsection (d)(3) within not less than 30 days before the effective date of such termination.

children.heading: Duties described and special rule for supplemental benefits
children.label: c Duties described and special rule for supplemental benefits
i
children.text: the determination of the amount of the discounted price of an applicable drug of a manufacturer;

children.label: ii
children.text: except as provided in clause (iii), the establishment of procedures under which discounted prices are provided to applicable beneficiaries at pharmacies or by mail order service at the point-of-sale of an applicable drug;

children.label: iii
children.text: in the case where, during the period beginning on , and ending on , it is not practicable to provide such discounted prices at the point-of-sale (as described in clause (ii)), the establishment of procedures to provide such discounted prices as soon as practicable after the point-of-sale;January 1, 2011December 31, 2011

children.label: I
children.text: the negotiated price of the applicable drug; and

children.label: II
children.text: the discounted price of the applicable drug;

children.label: v
children.text: the establishment of procedures to ensure that the discounted price for an applicable drug under this section is applied before any coverage or financial assistance under other health benefit plans or programs that provide coverage or financial assistance for the purchase or provision of prescription drug coverage on behalf of applicable beneficiaries as the Secretary may specify;

children.label: vi
children.text: the establishment of procedures to implement the special rule for supplemental benefits under paragraph (2); and

children.label: vii
children.text: providing a reasonable dispute resolution mechanism to resolve disagreements between manufacturers, applicable beneficiaries, and the third party with a contract under subsection (d)(3).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary shall monitor compliance by a manufacturer with the terms of an agreement under this section.

children.heading: Notification
children.label: ii Notification
children.text: If a third party with a contract under subsection (d)(3) determines that the manufacturer is not in compliance with such agreement, the third party shall notify the Secretary of such noncompliance for appropriate enforcement under subsection (e).

children.heading: Collection of data from prescription drug plans and MA–PD plans
children.label: C Collection of data from prescription drug plans and MA–PD plans
children.text: The Secretary may collect appropriate data from prescription drug plans and MA–PD plans in a timeframe that allows for discounted prices to be provided for applicable drugs under this section.

children.heading: Special rule for supplemental benefits
children.label: 2 Special rule for supplemental benefits
children.text: For plan year 2011 and each subsequent plan year, in the case where an applicable beneficiary has supplemental benefits with respect to applicable drugs under the prescription drug plan or MA–PD plan that the applicable beneficiary is enrolled in, the applicable beneficiary shall not be provided a discounted price for an applicable drug under this section until after such supplemental benefits have been applied with respect to the applicable drug.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), the Secretary shall provide for the implementation of this section, including the performance of the duties described in subsection (c)(1).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), in providing for such implementation, the Secretary shall not receive or distribute any funds of a manufacturer under the program.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: B Exception
children.text: The limitation under subparagraph (A) shall not apply to the Secretary with respect to drugs dispensed during the period beginning on , and ending on , but only if the Secretary determines that the exception to such limitation under this subparagraph is necessary in order for the Secretary to begin implementation of this section and provide applicable beneficiaries timely access to discounted prices during such period.January 1, 2011December 31, 2011

children.label: A
children.text: receive and transmit information between the Secretary, manufacturers, and other individuals or entities the Secretary determines appropriate;

children.label: B
children.text: receive, distribute, or facilitate the distribution of funds of manufacturers to appropriate individuals or entities in order to meet the obligations of manufacturers under agreements under this section;

children.label: C
children.text: provide adequate and timely information to manufacturers, consistent with the agreement with the manufacturer under this section, as necessary for the manufacturer to fulfill its obligations under this section; and

children.label: D
children.text: permit manufacturers to conduct periodic audits, directly or through contracts, of the data and information used by the third party to determine discounts for applicable drugs of the manufacturer under the program.

children.heading: Performance requirements
children.label: 4 Performance requirements
children.text: The Secretary shall establish performance requirements for a third party with a contract under paragraph (3) and safeguards to protect the independence and integrity of the activities carried out by the third party under the program under this section.

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: 5 Implementation
children.text: The Secretary may implement the program under this section by program instruction or otherwise.

children.heading: Administration
children.label: 6 Administration
children.text: Chapter 35 of title 44 shall not apply to the program under this section.

children.heading: Audits
children.label: 1 Audits
children.text: Each manufacturer with an agreement in effect under this section shall be subject to periodic audit by the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: the amount that the manufacturer would have paid with respect to such discounts under the agreement, which will then be used to pay the discounts which the manufacturer had failed to provide; and

children.label: ii
children.text: 25 percent of such amount.

children.heading: Application
children.label: B Application
children.text: The provisions of (other than subsections (a) and (b)) shall apply to a civil money penalty under this paragraph in the same manner as such provisions apply to a penalty or proceeding under .section 1320a–7a of this titlesection 1320a–7a(a) of this title

children.heading: Clarification regarding availability of other covered part D drugs
children.label: f Clarification regarding availability of other covered part D drugs
children.text: Nothing in this section shall prevent an applicable beneficiary from purchasing a covered part D drug that is not an applicable drug (including a generic drug or a drug that is not on the formulary of the prescription drug plan or MA–PD plan that the applicable beneficiary is enrolled in).

children.label: A
children.text: is enrolled in a prescription drug plan or an MA–PD plan;

children.label: B
children.text: is not enrolled in a qualified retiree prescription drug plan;

children.label: C
children.text: is not entitled to an income-related subsidy under ; andsection 1395w–114(a) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: has reached or exceeded the initial coverage limit under during the year; andsection 1395w–102(b)(3) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: has not incurred costs for covered part D drugs in the year equal to the annual out-of-pocket threshold specified in .section 1395w–102(b)(4)(B) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: approved under a new drug application under or, in the case of a biologic product, licensed under (other than, with respect to a plan year before 2019, a product licensed under subsection (k) of such section 262); andsection 355(b) of title 21section 262 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: if the PDP sponsor of the prescription drug plan or the MA organization offering the MA–PD plan uses a formulary, which is on the formulary of the prescription drug plan or MA–PD plan that the applicable beneficiary is enrolled in;

children.label: ii
children.text: if the PDP sponsor of the prescription drug plan or the MA organization offering the MA–PD plan does not use a formulary, for which benefits are available under the prescription drug plan or MA–PD plan that the applicable beneficiary is enrolled in; or

children.label: iii
children.text: is provided through an exception or appeal.

children.label: A
children.text: with respect to claims for reimbursement submitted electronically, 14 days; and

children.label: B
children.text: with respect to claims for reimbursement submitted otherwise, 30 days.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “discounted price” means 50 percent (or, with respect to a plan year after plan year 2018, 30 percent) of the negotiated price of the applicable drug of a manufacturer.

children.heading: Clarification
children.label: B Clarification
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed as affecting the responsibility of an applicable beneficiary for payment of a dispensing fee for an applicable drug.

children.heading: Special case for certain claims
children.label: C Special case for certain claims
children.text: In the case where the entire amount of the negotiated price of an individual claim for an applicable drug with respect to an applicable beneficiary does not fall at or above the initial coverage limit under and below the annual out-of-pocket threshold specified in for the year, the manufacturer of the applicable drug shall provide the discounted price under this section on only the portion of the negotiated price of the applicable drug that falls at or above such initial coverage limit and below such annual out-of-pocket threshold.section 1395w–102(b)(3) of this titlesection 1395w–102(b)(4)(B) of this title

children.heading: Manufacturer
children.label: 5 Manufacturer
children.text: The term “manufacturer” means any entity which is engaged in the production, preparation, propagation, compounding, conversion, or processing of prescription drug products, either directly or indirectly by extraction from substances of natural origin, or independently by means of chemical synthesis, or by a combination of extraction and chemical synthesis. Such term does not include a wholesale distributor of drugs or a retail pharmacy licensed under State law.

children.heading: Negotiated price
children.label: 6 Negotiated price
children.text: The term “negotiated price” has the meaning given such term in section 423.100 of title 42, Code of Federal Regulations (as in effect on ), except that such negotiated price shall not include any dispensing fee for the applicable drug.March 23, 2010

children.heading: Qualified retiree prescription drug plan
children.label: 7 Qualified retiree prescription drug plan
children.text: The term “qualified retiree prescription drug plan” has the meaning given such term in .section 1395w–132(a)(2) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The program shall not apply with respect to applicable drugs dispensed on or after , and, subject to paragraph (2), agreements under this section shall be terminated as of such date.January 1, 2025

children.heading: Continued application for applicable drugs dispensed prior to sunset
children.label: 2 Continued application for applicable drugs dispensed prior to sunset
children.text: The provisions of this section (including all responsibilities and duties) shall continue to apply on and after , with respect to applicable drugs dispensed prior to such date.January 1, 2025
